summary: A compact two-story residential structure with a gable roof. The building features brick and stone walls with horizontal banding, multiple small windows with glass panes, and a white concrete foundation/platform. The roof is steeply pitched with dark wood or dark brick material.

shape.overall_form: rectangular box with pitched gable roof

elements: no visible entrance in provided views
rebuild_hints: Build 8x8 rectangular footprint with 2-block-high foundation platform of concrete
Construct walls using brick with horizontal stonebrick bands separating floors
Add small window openings (approximately 2 blocks wide, 2 blocks tall) with glass panes and concrete frames
Create steep gable roof using dark wood or dark brick blocks, pitched at approximately 45 degrees
Ensure bilateral symmetry on front and back facades
Windows appear on both floors; upper floor has 3 windows visible, lower floor has 2-3 windows
Use concrete or light-colored blocks for window frames and foundation trim
uncertainties: Exact window count and placement on rear/side faces not fully visible
Precise roof pitch angle uncertain from isometric views
Interior layout and room configuration unknown
Entrance location not visible in any provided image
Exact block-by-block dimensions estimated from visual proportions
Whether roof is wood planks or dark brick blocks - appears dark but material unclear
